devanagari: ययातिचरितम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । यतिर्ययातिः संयातिः आयतिर्वियतिः कृतिः । षडिमे नहुषस्यासन् इन्द्रियाणीव देहिनः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: yayāticaritam śrīśuka uvāca yatiryayātiḥ saṃyātiḥ āyatirviyatiḥ kṛtiḥ ṣaḍime nahuṣasyāsan indriyāṇīva dehinaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Just as an embodied being is endowed with six senses (five senses plus the inner organ, the mind) King Nahuṣa had the following six sons—Yati, Yayāti, Saṃyāti, Āyati, Viyati and Kṛti.

devanagari: राज्यं नैच्छद् यतिः पित्रा दत्तं तत्परिणामवित् । यत्र प्रविष्टः पुरुष आत्मानं नावबुध्यते ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: rājyaṃ naicchad yatiḥ pitrā dattaṃ tatpariṇāmavit yatra praviṣṭaḥ puruṣa ātmānaṃ nāvabudhyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj ]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rāj ], [accusative single from √ rāj ]
[present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: Being aware of theconsequences of the acceptance of the rulership of a kingdom, Yati did not desire sovereignty offered to him by his father, for (he knew that) by shouldering kingship, a person does not realize his own self.

devanagari: पितरि भ्रंशिते स्थानाद् इन्द्राण्या धर्षणाद् द्विजैः । प्रापितेऽजगरत्वं वै ययातिरभवन्नृपः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: pitari bhraṃśite sthānād indrāṇyā dharṣaṇād dvijaiḥ prāpite

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ bhraṃś ], [vocative dual from √ bhraṃś ], [accusative dual from √ bhraṃś ], [locative single from √ bhraṃś ]
[nominative dual from √ bhraṃś ], [vocative single from √ bhraṃś ], [vocative dual from √ bhraṃś ], [accusative dual from √ bhraṃś ]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: When, due to his misdemeanour towards Indra’s queen, Indrāṇī, Nahuṣa was dethroned from his position (as the ruler of the celestial world) and was converted verily into a boa-constrictor by the Brāhmaṇa-sages, Yayāti became a king.

devanagari: चतसृष्वादिशद् दिक्षु भ्रातॄन् भ्राता यवीयसः । कृतदारो जुगोपोर्वीं काव्यस्य वृषपर्वणः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: catasṛṣvādiśad dikṣu bhrātṝn bhrātā yavīyasaḥ kṛtadāro jugoporvīṃ kāvyasya vṛṣaparvaṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Appointing his four younger brothers to govern over the kingdom in four directions (each in one direction), he married the daughter of Śukra (the preceptor of Asuras) and of Vṛṣaparvā, the king of Asuras and thus protected the earth (from his capital).

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । ब्रह्मर्षिर्भगवान् काव्यः क्षत्रबन्धुश्च नाहुषः । राजन्यविप्रयोः कस्माद् विवाहः प्रतिलोमकः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca brahmarṣirbhagavān kāvyaḥ kṣatrabandhuśca nāhuṣaḥ rājanyaviprayoḥ kasmād vivāhaḥ pratilomakaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
King Parīkṣit enquired:
The venerable Śukra was a Brāhmaṇa sage while Yayāti, the son of king Nahuṣa, was a Kṣattriya by caste. Why and how could such an irregular marriage against the caste-order between a Kṣatriya man and a Brāhmaṇa girl come about?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एकदा दानवेन्द्रस्य शर्मिष्ठा नाम कन्यका । सखीसहस्रसंयुक्ता गुरुपुत्र्या च भामिनी ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ekadā dānavendrasya śarmiṣṭhā nāma kanyakā sakhīsahasrasaṃyuktā guruputryā ca bhāminī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka replied:
On one occasion, in the company of a thousand lady-friends and Devayānī, the preceptor’s daughter, Śarmiṣṭhā, the princess of the great Dānava king Vṛṣaparvā who, though of the weaker sex was imperious by nature, was promenading in the pleasure garden of her capital which was full of blooming flower-trees, and charming with pools, the sandy banks and lotus beds of which were full of sweet humming of bees.

devanagari: देवयान्या पुरोद्याने पुष्पितद्रुमसंकुले । व्यचरत् कलगीतालि नलिनीपुलिनेऽबला ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: devayānyā purodyāne puṣpitadrumasaṃkule vyacarat kalagītāli nalinīpuline
translation:
Śrī Śuka replied:
On one occasion, in the company of a thousand lady-friends and Devayānī, the preceptor’s daughter, Śarmiṣṭhā, the princess of the great Dānava king Vṛṣaparvā who, though of the weaker sex was imperious by nature, was promenading in the pleasure garden of her capital which was full of blooming flower-trees, and charming with pools, the sandy banks and lotus beds of which were full of sweet humming of bees.

devanagari: ता जलाशयम आसाद्य कन्याः कमललोचनाः । तीरे न्यस्य दुकूलानि विजह्रुः सिञ्चतीर्मिथः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tā jalāśayama āsādya kanyāḥ kamalalocanāḥ tīre nyasya dukūlāni vijahruḥ siñcatīrmithaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sic class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sic class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sic class 6 verb], [locative single from √ sic class 6 verb]

translation: Arriving at a pool of water, those lotus-eyed girls left their silken garments on its bank and engaged themselves in water sports sprinkling each other with water.

devanagari: वीक्ष्य व्रजन्तं गिरिशं सह देव्या वृषस्थितम् । सहसोत्तीर्य वासांसि पर्यधुर्व्रीडिताः स्त्रियः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vīkṣya vrajantaṃ giriśaṃ saha devyā vṛṣasthitam sahasottīrya vāsāṃsi paryadhurvrīḍitāḥ striyaḥ

translation: Perceiving that god Śiva accompanied by his Consort Pārvatī, was riding that way on his bull, the ladies forthwith rushed out of the water, and hastened to put on their garments, out of bashfulness.

devanagari: शर्मिष्ठाजानती वासो गुरुपुत्र्याः समव्ययत् । स्वीयं मत्वा प्रकुपिता देवयानी इदमब्रवीत् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: śarmiṣṭhājānatī vāso guruputryāḥ samavyayat svīyaṃ matvā prakupitā devayānī idamabravīt

translation: Not knowing that the garments belonged to the preceptor’s daughter, Śarmiṣṭhā wore it, thinking it to be her own. Getting enraged thereby, Devayānī spoke as follows:

devanagari: अहो निरीक्ष्यतामस्या दास्याः कर्म ह्यसाम्प्रतम् । अस्मद्धार्यं धृतवती शुनीव हविरध्वरे ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: aho nirīkṣyatāmasyā dāsyāḥ karma hyasāmpratam asmaddhāryaṃ dhṛtavatī śunīva haviradhvare

[nominative single from √ hṝ class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ hṝ class 9 verb]

translation: “Oh! Behold the impropriety of this slave-girl in that, like a bitch carrying away the oblation (meant for gods) in a sacrifice, she wore the garment which was to be put on by us.

devanagari: यैरिदं तपसा सृष्टं मुखं पुंसः परस्य ये । धार्यते यैरिह ज्योतिः शिवः पन्थाः प्रदर्शितः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yairidaṃ tapasā sṛṣṭaṃ mukhaṃ puṃsaḥ parasya ye dhāryate yairiha jyotiḥ śivaḥ panthāḥ pradarśitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: By us Brāhmaṇas (like god Brahmā, Prajāpatis like Marīci) has been created this universe through the power of our asceticism and penance; we constitute the mouth of the Supreme Person (i.e. we are born from god’s mouth and as such are superior to all, as he gets propitiated when we are feasted to our satisfaction). By us the light of the Supreme Brahman is conserved (as an object of adoration) in our hearts, and the blessed path of Vedic religion is shown by us.

devanagari: यान् वन्दन्ति उपतिष्ठन्ते लोकनाथाः सुरेश्वराः । भगवानपि विश्वात्मा पावनः श्रीनिकेतनः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: yān vandanti upatiṣṭhante lokanāthāḥ sureśvarāḥ bhagavānapi viśvātmā pāvanaḥ śrīniketanaḥ
translation: To us (Brāhmaṇas) the Lords of gods, protectors of the world, and even the most purifying, glorious Lord Viṣṇu, the Soul of the Universe and the abode of goddess Śrī, pay homage and praise.

devanagari: वयं तत्रापि भृगवः शिष्योऽस्या नः पितासुरः । अस्मद्धार्यं धृतवती शूद्रो वेदमिवासती ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ tatrāpi bhṛgavaḥ śiṣyo asmaddhāryaṃ dhṛtavatī śūdro vedamivāsatī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛ ]
[nominative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ hṛ ], [accusative single from √ hṛ ]
[nominative single from √ hṛ class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ hṝ class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ hṝ class 9 verb]

translation: And among such Brāhmaṇas, we are the descendants of Bhṛgu. Her father, a demon, is our disciple. This unchaste girl has put on the dress meant for us like a Śūdra bearing (reciting) the Veda—(a privilege of the twice-born castes).

devanagari: एवं क्षिपन्तीं शर्मिष्ठा गुरुपुत्रीं अभाषत । रुषा श्वसन्ति उरंगीघ्गीव धर्षिता दष्टदच्छदा ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ kṣipantīṃ śarmiṣṭhā guruputrīṃ abhāṣata ruṣā śvasanti uraṃgīghgīva dharṣitā daṣṭadacchadā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śvas class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śvas class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ śvas class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ dhṛṣ ], [vocative plural from √ dhṛṣ ]
[nominative plural from √ dhṛṣ ], [vocative plural from √ dhṛṣ ], [accusative plural from √ dhṛṣ ]

translation: Thus attacked, Śarmiṣṭhā who angrily sighed heavily like a hissing female serpent, bit her lips and retorted to the spiritual preceptor’s daughter who was reproaching her with words of abuse.

devanagari: आत्मवृत्तमविज्ञाय कत्थसे बहु भिक्षुकि । किं न प्रतीक्षसेऽस्माकं गृहान् बलिभुजो यथा ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmavṛttamavijñāya katthase bahu bhikṣuki kiṃ na pratīkṣase

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb]

translation: “Oh! Beggar-maid, ignoring your own (menial) position, you are vainly boasting yourself. Do you not wait at our house-(door) like a crow or a dog (for crumbs of food)?”

devanagari: एवंविधैः सुपरुषैः क्षिप्त्वाऽचार्यसुतां सतीम् । शर्मिष्ठा प्राक्षिपत् कूपे वासे आदाय मन्युना ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃvidhaiḥ suparuṣaiḥ kṣiptvā śarmiṣṭhā prākṣipat kūpe vāse ādāya manyunā
translation: Having reproached the good daughter of her preceptor with such and other harsh words, Śarmiṣṭhā snatched away all her cloths and pushed her into a well.

devanagari: तस्यां गतायां स्वगृहं ययातिर्मृगयां चरन् । प्राप्तो यदृच्छया कूपे जलार्थी तां ददर्श ह ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ gatāyāṃ svagṛhaṃ yayātirmṛgayāṃ caran prāpto yadṛcchayā kūpe jalārthī tāṃ dadarśa ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: It is traditionally told that when Śarmiṣṭhā returned home, Yayāti who was on the hunting expedition, wanted to drink water, and happened, by chance, to come to that well where he saw her (Devayānī).

devanagari: दत्त्वा स्वमुत्तरं वासः तस्यै राजा विवाससे । गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणिं उज्जहार दयापरः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: dattvā svamuttaraṃ vāsaḥ tasyai rājā vivāsase gṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇiṃ ujjahāra dayāparaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative middle first single], [imperative passive first single]

translation: The merciful king gave his upper garment to the girl who was stark naked, and holding her hand by his own, he lifted her out of water.

devanagari: तं वीरमाहौशनसी प्रेमनिर्भरया गिरा । राजन् त्वया गृहीतो मे पाणिः परपुरञ्जय ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vīramāhauśanasī premanirbharayā girā rājan tvayā gṛhīto me pāṇiḥ parapurañjaya

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Devayānī, the daughter of Uśanas (i. e. Śukra) spoke to the king in words steeped in genuine love: “Oh King! You have accepted my hand, Oh conqueror of inimical cities!”

devanagari: हस्तग्राहोऽपरो मा भूद् गृहीतायास्त्वया हि मे । एष ईशकृतो वीर सम्बन्धो नौ न पौरुषः । यदिदं कूपमग्नाया भवतो दर्शनं मम ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: hastagrāho eṣa īśakṛto vīra sambandho nau na pauruṣaḥ yadidaṃ kūpamagnāyā bhavato darśanaṃ mama

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ grah class 9 verb], [genitive single from √ grah class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: “As my hand has now been accepted by you once, let no one else now take it. This relation of ours is ordained providentially and not through human agency, Oh valiant warrior. It is hence that I could have this sight of yours while clinging to the (walls of the) well.

devanagari: न ब्राह्मणो मे भविता हस्तग्राहो महाभुज । कचस्य बार्हस्पत्यस्य शापाद् यमशपं पुरा ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: na brāhmaṇo me bhavitā hastagrāho mahābhuja kacasya bārhaspatyasya śāpād yamaśapaṃ purā
translation: Oh King of powerful arms! No Brāhmaṇa is destined to accept my hand due to the (reciprocal curse of Kaca the son of Bṛhaspati whom I imprecated initially”.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
According to Mahābhārata Ādi. chs. 76-77 Kaca, the son of Bṛhaspati could successfully learn the Sañjīvanī Vidyā from Śukra through the special favour of Devayānī. At the time of departure Devayānī proposed to marry him. But he rejected her on the ground that being his preceptor’s daughter she is like a sister to him. Enraged at this, Devayānī cursed him that his vidyā will be futile. Kaca reciprocated the curse (that no Brāhmaṇa would marry her).

devanagari: ययातिरनभिप्रेतं दैवोपहृतमात्मनः । मनस्तु तद्‍गतं बुद्ध्वा प्रतिजग्राह तद्वचः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yayātiranabhipretaṃ daivopahṛtamātmanaḥ manastu tad‍gataṃ buddhvā pratijagrāha tadvacaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Yayāti found that the unexpected event was brought about by his destiny and being conscious that now his heart was attached to her, accepted her proposal.

devanagari: गते राजनि सा धीरे तत्र स्म रुदती पितुः । न्यवेदयत् ततः सर्वं उक्तं शर्मिष्ठया कृतम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: gate rājani sā dhīre tatra sma rudatī pituḥ nyavedayat tataḥ sarvaṃ uktaṃ śarmiṣṭhayā kṛtam

translation: When the valiant king departed, Devayānī went to her father crying (all the while) and reported to him all that was said and done by Śarmiṣṭhā.

devanagari: दुर्मना भगवान् काव्यः पौरोहित्यं विगर्हयन् । स्तुवन् वृत्तिं च कापोतीं दुहित्रा स ययौ पुरात् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: durmanā bhagavān kāvyaḥ paurohityaṃ vigarhayan stuvan vṛttiṃ ca kāpotīṃ duhitrā sa yayau purāt

translation: The glorious sage Śukra was deeply agitated at heart. Disparaging the profession of a priest and commending the way of life of a pigeon (which freely gathers its food in the forest—technically known as Uñccha-vṛtti—“maintenance by gleaning”), he went out of the city (Vṛṣaparvā’s capital) along with his daughter.

devanagari: वृषपर्वा तमाज्ञाय प्रत्यनीक विवक्षितम् । गुरुं प्रसादयन् मूर्ध्ना पादयोः पतितः पथि ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛṣaparvā tamājñāya pratyanīka vivakṣitam guruṃ prasādayan mūrdhnā pādayoḥ patitaḥ pathi

translation: Knowing that his intention is to conspire and bring victory to the enemies viz. gods, Vṛṣaparvā propitiated his preceptor by falling prostrate at his feet on the way.

devanagari: क्षणार्ध मन्युर्भगवान् शिष्यं व्याचष्ट भार्गवः । कामोऽस्याः क्रियतां राजन् नैनां त्यक्तुमिहोत्सहे ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣaṇārdha manyurbhagavān śiṣyaṃ vyācaṣṭa bhārgavaḥ kāmo

translation: The glorious Śukra, the descendent of Bhṛgu whose anger lasts hardly for half a moment, spoke to his disciple Vṛṣaparvā, “Oh King! Let the desire of her (Devayānī) be satisfied because I cannot brook to let her alone.”

devanagari: तथेति अवस्थिते प्राह देवयानी मनोगतम् । पित्रा दत्ता यतो यास्ये सानुगा यातु मामनु ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tatheti avasthite prāha devayānī manogatam pitrā dattā yato yāsye sānugā yātu māmanu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

[nominative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ yas class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ yas class 4 verb]

translation: When Vṛṣaparvā agreed with the words, “Be it so”, and awaited (to hear Devayānī’s desire), Devayānī disclosed what was in her mind. “Wherever I shall go after being given away by my father in marriage, let her (Śarmiṣṭhā) follow me along with her companions (as attendants).”

devanagari: स्वानां तत् संकटं वीक्ष्य तदर्थस्य च गौरवम् । देवयानीं पर्यचरत् स्त्रीसहस्रेण दासवत् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: svānāṃ tat saṃkaṭaṃ vīkṣya tadarthasya ca gauravam devayānīṃ paryacarat strīsahasreṇa dāsavat
translation: Realizing the peril (involved in the departure of Śukra) to her people, and the importance of the matter, she (Śarmiṣṭhā) along with her thousand laḍy-companions, attended upon Devayānī like a menial.

devanagari: नाहुषाय सुतां दत्त्वा सह शर्मिष्ठयोशना । तमाह राजन् शर्मिष्ठां आधास्तल्पे न कर्हिचित् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: nāhuṣāya sutāṃ dattvā saha śarmiṣṭhayośanā tamāha rājan śarmiṣṭhāṃ ādhāstalpe na karhicit

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: Having given in marriage his daughter along with Śarmiṣṭhā to Yayāti, the son of Nahuṣa, Śukra warned him, “Oh king! Never take Śarmiṣṭhā to share the bed with you.”

devanagari: विलोक्यौशनसीं राजन् शर्मिष्ठा सप्रजां क्वचित् । तमेव वव्रे रहसि सख्याः पतिमृतौ सती ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: vilokyauśanasīṃ rājan śarmiṣṭhā saprajāṃ kvacit tameva vavre rahasi sakhyāḥ patimṛtau satī

translation: On one occasion, seeing Devayānī blessed with a son, the chaste lady Śarmiṣṭhā, at the period proper for conception, courted her friend’s (Devayānī’s) husband secretly (for children).

devanagari: राजपुत्र्यार्थितोऽपत्ये धर्मं चावेक्ष्य धर्मवित् । स्मरन् शुक्रवचः काले दिष्टमेवाभ्यपद्यत ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: rājaputryārthito smaran śukravacaḥ kāle diṣṭamevābhyapadyata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: The king, being well-versed in Dharma-śāstra, realized it to be his sacred duty to comply with the request of the princess (Śarmiṣṭhā) who solicited him for progeny, and he followed the course ordained for him by Destiny, though he remembered the warning of Śukra.

devanagari: यदुं च तुर्वसुं चैव देवयानी व्यजायत । द्रुह्युं चानुं च पूरुं च शर्मिष्ठा वार्षपर्वणी ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yaduṃ ca turvasuṃ caiva devayānī vyajāyata druhyuṃ cānuṃ ca pūruṃ ca śarmiṣṭhā vārṣaparvaṇī
translation: Devayānī gave birth to Yadu and Turvasu, while Śarmiṣṭhā, the daughter of Vṛṣaparvā, had three sons—Druhyu, Anu, and Pūru.

devanagari: गर्भसंभवमासुर्या भर्तुर्विज्ञाय मानिनी । देवयानी पितुर्गेहं ययौ क्रोधविमूर्छिता ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: garbhasaṃbhavamāsuryā bharturvijñāya māninī devayānī piturgehaṃ yayau krodhavimūrchitā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Learning about the pregnancy of the Asura princess from her husband, proud Devayānī, being offended with her husband (through jealousy and pride), was overwhelmed with rage and proceeded to her father’s house.

devanagari: प्रियां अनुगतः कामी वचोभिः उपमन्त्रयन् । न प्रसादयितुं शेके पादसंवाहनादिभिः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: priyāṃ anugataḥ kāmī vacobhiḥ upamantrayan na prasādayituṃ śeke pādasaṃvāhanādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ mantr class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ mantr class 10 verb]

translation: The passionate king followed his beloved to reconcile her with words of appeasement and winsome promises, but was not able to please her, even by shampooing her feet.

devanagari: शुक्रस्तमाह कुपितः स्त्रीकामानृतपूरुष । त्वां जरा विशतां मन्द विरूपकरणी नृणाम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: śukrastamāha kupitaḥ strīkāmānṛtapūruṣa tvāṃ jarā viśatāṃ manda virūpakaraṇī nṛṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ kup class 10 verb]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: Flying in rage, Śukra cursed him, “You liar! Woman-addict! Let the decrepitude of old age which disfigures men overtake you, you stupid fellow!”

devanagari: श्रीययातिरुवाच । अतृप्तोऽस्म्यद्य कामानां ब्रह्मन् दुहितरि स्म ते । व्यत्यस्यतां यथाकामं वयसा योऽभिधास्यति ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīyayātiruvāca atṛpto vyatyasyatāṃ yathākāmaṃ vayasā yo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative passive third single]

translation: Yayāti urged, “Oh Brāhmaṇa Sage! I am not till today sated with enjoyment of pleasure with your daughter”. (Śukra replied) “In that case, you may exchange it (your old age) with the (youth of) anyone else who is willing to accept it.”

devanagari: इति लब्धव्यवस्थानः पुत्रं ज्येष्ठमवोचत । यदो तात प्रतीच्छेमां जरां देहि निजं वयः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: iti labdhavyavasthānaḥ putraṃ jyeṣṭhamavocata yado tāta pratīcchemāṃ jarāṃ dehi nijaṃ vayaḥ
translation: Having secured this arrangement (as a way out of the curse), Yayāti addressed to his eldest son: “Oh my child Yadu! Please accept this decrepitude of age caused by your maternal grand-father, and give me your youth in exchange. Dear child! I am not yet satisfied with enjoyment of pleasures. I would like to enjoy for a few more years, with your youth.”

devanagari: मातामहकृतां वत्स न तृप्तो विषयेष्वहम् । वयसा भवदीयेन रंस्ये कतिपयाः समाः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: mātāmahakṛtāṃ vatsa na tṛpto viṣayeṣvaham vayasā bhavadīyena raṃsye katipayāḥ samāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[nominative single from √ tṛp class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ tṛp class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ tṛp class 6 verb]

translation: Having secured this arrangement (as a way out of the curse), Yayāti addressed to his eldest son: “Oh my child Yadu! Please accept this decrepitude of age caused by your maternal grand-father, and give me your youth in exchange. Dear child! I am not yet satisfied with enjoyment of pleasures. I would like to enjoy for a few more years, with your youth.”

devanagari: श्रीयदुरुवाच । नोत्सहे जरसा स्थातुं अन्तरा प्राप्तया तव । अविदित्वा सुखं ग्राम्यं वैतृष्ण्यं नैति पूरुषः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīyaduruvāca notsahe jarasā sthātuṃ antarā prāptayā tava aviditvā sukhaṃ grāmyaṃ vaitṛṣṇyaṃ naiti pūruṣaḥ
translation:
Yadu replied:
[2] “I dare not accept and live with the old age which overtook you in the middle of life, (for) a man cannot attain to desirelessness without an actual experience of the worldly pleasures first.”
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Yadu was very pious (infra 10.1.2). VC defends Yadu’s disobedience thus: Yadu wanted to devote himself to the services of the Lord at the earliest. That mental stage is possible only after actual experience of worldly pleasures for some days. Acceptance of Yayāti’s age would postpone that mental stage and consequently the life devoted to the services of the Lord. Between Service to the Lord and disobedience to father, the former is preferable according to Dharma. Marvellous defence!

devanagari: तुर्वसुश्चोदितः पित्रा द्रुह्युश्चानुश्च भारत । प्रत्याचख्युरधर्मज्ञा ह्यनित्ये नित्यबुद्धयः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: turvasuścoditaḥ pitrā druhyuścānuśca bhārata pratyācakhyuradharmajñā hyanitye nityabuddhayaḥ
translation: Turvasu, Anu, and Druhyu were then requested (one by one) by the father. But they did not know what was righteousness and regarded transient things (like youth) as eternal, and refused his proposal. Oh scion of Bharata.

devanagari: अपृच्छत् तनयं पूरुं वयसोनं गुणाधिकम् । न त्वं अग्रजवद् वत्स मां प्रत्याख्यातुमर्हसि ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: apṛcchat tanayaṃ pūruṃ vayasonaṃ guṇādhikam na tvaṃ agrajavad vatsa māṃ pratyākhyātumarhasi
translation: He requested his son Pūru who was the youngest in age but senior-most in virtues. “Oh child! It does not behove you to refuse my request as was done by your elder brothers”.

devanagari: श्रीपूरुरुवाच । को नु लोके मनुष्येन्द्र पितुरात्मकृतः पुमान् । प्रतिकर्तुं क्षमो यस्य प्रसादाद् विन्दते परम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīpūruruvāca ko nu loke manuṣyendra piturātmakṛtaḥ pumān pratikartuṃ kṣamo yasya prasādād vindate param

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Pūru said:
“Oh lord of men! What man on earth is capable of repaying fully the obligations of his father, the procreator of his body, and through whose grace one can attain the highest (objective in life)?

devanagari: उत्तमश्चिन्तितं कुर्यात् प्रोक्तकारी तु मध्यमः । अधमोऽश्रद्धया कुर्याद् अकर्तोच्चरितं पितुः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: uttamaścintitaṃ kuryāt proktakārī tu madhyamaḥ adhamo
translation: The best son is he who accomplishes the task in anticipation of the expectation of his father. The mediocre is one who complies when expressly told by his father. The lowest type is he who obeys but with irreverence. And he who disobeys is the excretion of his father.”

devanagari: इति प्रमुदितः पूरुः प्रत्यगृह्णाज्जरां पितुः । सोऽपि तद्वयसा कामान् यथावज्जुजुषे नृप ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti pramuditaḥ pūruḥ pratyagṛhṇājjarāṃ pituḥ so
translation: In this way, Pūru gladly accepted the old age of his father. With his youth, Yayāti also enjoyed pleasures at his sweet will.

devanagari: सप्तद्वीपपतिः सम्यक् पितृवत् पालयन् प्रजाः । यथोपजोषं विषयान् जुजुषेऽव्याहतेन्द्रियः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: saptadvīpapatiḥ samyak pitṛvat pālayan prajāḥ yathopajoṣaṃ viṣayān jujuṣe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pāl class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ pāl class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ pā ], [vocative single from √ pā ]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: As a lord of the seven continents of the earth, he protected his subjects well like a father, and with his senses unimpaired, he gratified his desires (by enjoying pleasures) to his heart’s content.

devanagari: देवयान्यप्यनुदिनं मनोवाग् देहवस्तुभिः । प्रेयसः परमां प्रीतिं उवाह प्रेयसी रहः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: devayānyapyanudinaṃ manovāg dehavastubhiḥ preyasaḥ paramāṃ prītiṃ uvāha preyasī rahaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: His beloved Devayānī every day heightened his love by affording pleasures to him by her thought, word and deed, and by offering objects of enjoyment in privacy.

devanagari: अयजद् यज्ञपुरुषं क्रतुभिर्भूरिदक्षिणैः । सर्वदेवमयं देवं सर्ववेदमयं हरिम् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: ayajad yajñapuruṣaṃ kratubhirbhūridakṣiṇaiḥ sarvadevamayaṃ devaṃ sarvavedamayaṃ harim
translation: By performance of sacrifices with profusely liberal gifts, Yayāti worshipped Lord Hari (Destroyer of all miseries and sins) the Presiding Deity of sacrifices, the God who represents all gods in Himself, and is an embodiment of all Vedas.

devanagari: यस्मिन्निदं विरचितं व्योम्नीव जलदावलिः । नानेव भाति नाभाति स्वप्नमायामनोरथः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: yasminnidaṃ viracitaṃ vyomnīva jaladāvaliḥ nāneva bhāti nābhāti svapnamāyāmanorathaḥ
translation: On Hari is superimposed this universe as a row of clouds on the sky and which, like a dream or an illusion or a fancy now appears to be different (in names, forms etc.) and now ceases to be (in dreamless sleep or after the deluge of the world).

devanagari: तमेव हृदि विन्यस्य वासुदेवं गुहाशयम् । नारायणमणीयांसं निराशीरयजत् प्रभुम् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: tameva hṛdi vinyasya vāsudevaṃ guhāśayam nārāyaṇamaṇīyāṃsaṃ nirāśīrayajat prabhum
translation: Enthroning in his heart the Almighty Lord Vāsudeva who is abiding in the hearts of all as the Indweller, he (Yayāti) worshipped the subtle-most Nārāyaṇa without cherishing any desire for blessings.

devanagari: एवं वर्षसहस्राणि मनःषष्ठैर्मनःसुखम् । विदधानोऽपि नातृप्यत् सार्वभौमः कदिन्द्रियैः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ varṣasahasrāṇi manaḥṣaṣṭhairmanaḥsukham vidadhāno
translation:
Yayāti, the emperor of the entire earth, did not feel sated even though he enjoyed pleasures for thousands of years through his (five) wicked senses of which his mind was the sixth.
